title: What is an Agent Content Tree?
summary: A two-minute primer on the ACT spec: nodes, summaries, blocks, and how an agent walks them.

content.text: An ACT tree is a graph of nodes. Every node carries a stable id, a title, a one-sentence summary, and zero or more typed content blocks (prose, data, marketing).

Agents fetch the manifest at /.well-known/act.json, then walk subtrees — each subtree shows the immediate descendants of a node as summary entries (no body) so the agent can budget before drilling in.
The body of any single node is a separate fetch. That's the whole disclosure pattern: walk summaries cheaply, fetch bodies deliberately.
